member.name: Peter Kyle
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Peter Kyle is the Labour MP for Hove and Portslade , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015. He currently holds the Government posts of Secretary of State for Business and Trade, and President of the Board of Trade.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Lung Cancer: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, pursuant to the Answer of 7 May 2024 to Question 24269 on Lung Cancer: Artificial Intelligence, which (a) NHS trusts have and (b) imaging network has not received funding.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since the initial announcements regarding funding for National Health Service trusts to integrate artificial intelligence into lung cancer imaging, adjustments have been made to the list of participants. Three trusts have withdrawn from the initiative, an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Government Departments: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, pursuant to the Answer of 22 February 2024 on Government Departments: Artificial Intelligence, whether the detailed policy for the Algorithmic transparency recording standard has been a
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are implementing the mandatory rollout of the ATRS in phases, with the current, first phase focusing on the 16 largest ministerial departments, plus HMRC. The Department of Science, Innovation and Technology and the Central Digital and Data Office are ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology: Public Expenditure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, pursuant to the Answer of 22 April 2024 to Question 22348 on Public Expenditure, what his planned timetable is for publishing the response to David Willetts’ report.
writtenQuestions.answerText: DSIT published the response to Lord Willetts’ independent review of the DSIT business case and approvals process on 1 May on gov.uk: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/reforming-the-dsit-business-case-process
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Elections
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether the AI Safety Institute plans to carry out testing on the societal impacts of AI on elections.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The AI Safety Institute has responsibility for developing and conducting evaluations on advanced AI systems, including assessing potentially harmful capabilities. As part of this, it will evaluate the direct impact of advanced AI systems on individuals an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Semiconductors: Infrastructure

writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK has a thriving R&D innovation semiconductor ecosystem and the Government’s National Semiconductor Strategy sets out our ambitious vision for the UK’s sector.The Government has commissioned the UK Semiconductor Infrastructure Initiative feasibility ...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Since the original announcement, two additional trusts have been included in this initiative, bringing the total to 66, from the previously announced 64. Funding to deploy artificial intelligence (AI) to speed up the diagnosis and treatment of lung cancer...

writtenQuestions.heading: Semiconductors: Infrastructure

writtenQuestions.answerText: We have made substantial progress since the launch of the National Semiconductor Strategy last year. We are on track to exceed our initial £200 million investment commitment for this spending review period, launched a semiconductor incubator Programme, Ch...

writtenQuestions.heading: Rosalind Franklin Laboratory
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what the cost was of the early exit from the lease for the Rosalind Franklin Laboratory.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The figures were released on Monday 21st May 2024 and are included in the link below:https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/ukhsa-spend-over-25000-2024
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: ICT: Edinburgh

writtenQuestions.answerText: Compute is a priority for this government, underpinning our ambitions for AI, science and technology and R&D. Following the October announcement that Exascale will be hosted in Edinburgh, we are taking forward market engagement to inform the delivery appr...

writtenQuestions.heading: Life Sciences: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what progress his Department has made on the £100 million AI Life Sciences Accelerator Mission.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Prime Minister announced a new artificial intelligence (AI) in healthcare fund, backed by £100 million, at the AI Safety Summit. The Department for Science, Innovation and Technology is responsible for this fund, and the Department of Health and Socia...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology: Public Expenditure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether she plans to respond to the recommendations of the independent report entitled Reforming the DSIT business case process, published by her Department on 9 February 2024.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government welcomes David Willetts’ report and is considering the recommendations, including options for implementation. We are planning to publish a response before the end of April.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: UK Research and Innovation: Social Media
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, with reference to Q1 of the oral evidence given to the House of Lords Science and Technology Select Committee on 12 March 2024, whether her Department (a) monitors and (b) records polit
writtenQuestions.answerText: No. Due diligence is sometimes carried out by officials, at specific times, for example appointments made directly by DSIT, consistent with standard cross-government approaches.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Migrant Workers
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, pursuant to the Answer of 11 March 2024 to Question 17194 on Artificial Intelligence: Migrant Workers, when the new dedicated visa scheme will be operational.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The new Future Technology Research & Innovation (Government Authorised Exchange) visa scheme is expected to go live on 4 April 2024, which the Home Office announced in their Statement of Changes to the Immigration Rules laid in Parliament on 14 March. Thi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Kate Sang
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, with reference to the opening statement by the Secretary of State at the Lords Science, Innovation and Technology Select Committee on 12 March 2024, what the total cost to the public pu
writtenQuestions.answerText: A sum of £15,000 was paid without admitting any liability. This approach is intended to reduce the overall costs to the taxpayer that could result from protracted legal action.Costs relating to legal fees incurred by the department will be published as pa...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: UK Research and Innovation: Social Media
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what her policy is on producing reports into the online activity of academics on UKRI boards.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Secretary of State is a staunch believer in free speech and would not authorise a sweeping collection of the views of academics.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology: Defamation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, with reference to paragraphs 7.16 and 7.17 of the Ministerial Code, on how many occasions Ministers in her Department have informed the Law Officers that they are the defendants in a li
writtenQuestions.answerText: As has been the practice under successive Governments, it is not the convention to comment on where the Law Officers have, or have not been, informed or advised on such legal matters. This is reflected in paragraph 2.13 of the Ministerial Code.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
